segment.source: Header

A standalone heading element with no body text — just a title and optional subheading you can size from H1 to H5. Use it to label or separate the sections that follow, or to drop in a styled heading where a full text element would be too much.
Text

The everyday rich-text element: a heading plus formatted body copy with paragraphs, lists, links and inline emphasis. Reach for it whenever you need words on the page and nothing more — it is the most-used content element in TYPO3.
Text & Images

A text element with one or more images wrapped beside or around the copy. Control image position (left, right, above, below), the number of columns and click-to-enlarge behaviour. Use it for the classic illustrated paragraph.
Text & Media

Like Text & Images, but the media column also accepts video and audio next to images, with the same layout, gallery and link options. Use it when a paragraph needs an embedded video or a mixed-media gallery.
Images Only

One or more images arranged in a grid of columns with optional captions and click-to-enlarge — no body text. Use it for a photo gallery, screenshots, logos or any set of pictures that should stand on their own.
Bullet List
A simple bullet list where every line of the body becomes one list item. Use it for short, scannable lists of features, steps or takeaways where rich formatting per item isn't needed.
Table

A simple data table you fill in a grid editor, with an optional header row or column, caption and delimiter. Use it for specs, comparisons, schedules or any tabular data — it stays responsive and accessible on the frontend.
File Links

A download list that turns attached files (or a file collection) into links, optionally showing file type, size and a preview thumbnail. Use it to offer PDFs, brochures, datasheets or any documents for download.
Divider
A visual divider that inserts a horizontal line or spacing between content elements. Use it to break a long column into clear sections without adding a heading or text.
Plain HTML

An element that outputs raw HTML exactly as you enter it, without processing. Use it for third-party embeds, custom markup or snippets that need to bypass the rich-text editor — for trusted editors only.
Insert Records

Embeds existing content elements (or other records) into this position instead of duplicating them. Use it to reuse one maintained block — a call to action, contact box or note — across several pages from a single source.
Menu: Selected Pages
A navigation menu that links to a list of pages you pick by hand. Use it for a curated set of links — a sub-navigation, a "see also" block or a hand-built section menu.
Menu: Subpages
A menu that automatically lists the subpages of the pages you select, so it updates itself as the page tree changes. Use it for an auto-maintained section or directory navigation.
Menu: Sitemap
An expanded, multi-level menu of all subpages of the current site — a classic HTML sitemap. Use it on a sitemap page to give visitors and crawlers a full overview of the page tree.
Menu: Sitemaps of Selected Pages
An expanded, multi-level menu of all subpages beneath the pages you select — a sitemap scoped to specific branches rather than the whole site. Use it to map out one or more sections in full.
Menu: Section Index
A menu built from the content elements on a page that are marked "index for section menus" — an in-page jump list. Use it as a table of contents that links to anchors within the same page.
Menu: Section Index of Selected Pages
A menu of the subpages of the pages you select, including each subpage's section-index content as sub-items. Use it for a deep navigation that surfaces both pages and their in-page sections.
Menu: Abstracts
A menu of the subpages of selected pages that shows each page's title together with its abstract or description text. Use it as a teaser-style index that previews where each link leads.
Menu: Recently Updated
A menu that lists the most recently changed pages, newest first. Use it to surface fresh content — a "what's new" block or a sidebar of recent updates.
Menu: Related Pages
A menu that automatically links to pages sharing keywords with the current page. Use it as a "related content" or "you might also like" block driven by each page's keywords.
Menu: Categorized Pages
A menu that lists all pages assigned to the categories you choose. Use it to build a category-driven index — for example all pages tagged "News" or "Products" — that updates automatically.
Menu: Categorized Content
A menu that collects content elements assigned to the categories you choose, from across the site. Use it to surface tagged content blocks — like all elements categorized "Downloads" — in one place.
Form

An interactive form built with TYPO3's Form Framework: pick a form definition and the element renders its fields, validation and confirmation. Use it for contact, registration or survey forms that submit data and send notifications.
Login Form
A login and logout form that password-protects pages, granting access only to authorised website users and groups. Use it on a members' area or intranet entry page to authenticate frontend users.
Form (Powermail)

An interactive form built with Powermail: select a configured form and the element renders its fields, multi-step pages, validation and email finishers. Use it for richer marketing and contact forms with conditional fields and reporting.
